_doc: DCE attribute schema for the unified preference scorer. Each attribute's `levels` are ordered REFERENCE (ladder 0) -> TARGET (ladder 1). The user's preference_vector[attr] = sigmoid(beta of the TARGET level vs the reference), so beta_hat = logit(v) > 0 means the user prefers the target level. Level ids are the canonical job-side `selected_level_id` values emitted by the occupations DB / llm_job_attributes; resolve_schema_level_id matches them directly (no alias needed). Ordering choices are validated by the directional tests in backend/tests/test_dce_utility.py.

attributes.name: earnings_per_month
attributes.label: Monthly Earnings
attributes.target_note: continuous earnings; higher = target. v>0.5 => prefers higher pay.

attributes.name: career_growth
attributes.label: Career Growth
attributes.target_note: value encodes growth_high part-worth. v>0.5 => prefers strong growth.

attributes.name: physical_demand
attributes.label: Physical Demand & Risk
attributes.target_note: value encodes phys_heavy part-worth (elicitation term physical_demand_phys_heavy). v<0.5 => dislikes heavy/risky work, so heavy jobs (ladder 1) get a negative contribution. CONFIRM against production mapping.

attributes.name: social_interaction
attributes.label: Social Interaction
attributes.target_note: ordered alone -> peers -> customers (increasing interaction). Under the current contract the user-side social_interaction value is typically 0.5 (neutral => no contribution); the exact target level the value encodes needs production confirmation.
